pages.full_text:
84 takeit in turns to lay the table, dry up & put away. This is a tremendous help & they do it very well.
	We tried one campsite, which looked from the map as if it would be on a lake: but, infact, they had drained the reservoir behind the dam, so there was just a dirty valley with ghostly dead trees in the bottom. So after an icecream, we went on, through beautiful farming country.
	We have ended up at this campsite. The pits. We are far from the lake, cheek by jowl with neighbours & parked on pine cones & dirty, which John & Charles keep throwing around.
[right margin, blue ink:] Lacopelle
[illegible] - Vieschamp
pages.notes: Page continues mid-narrative from previous spread. 'infact' written as one word as in original. The right margin contains two annotations in lighter blue ink, likely campsite names or place labels: first reads 'Lacopelle' fairly clearly; the second line reads '[illegible] - Vieschamp' — the first word before the dash is not clearly legible, possibly a first name or prefix. 'Vieschamp' could alternatively read 'Vieschamps' or 'Vieschamp'. The 'throwing around' at the bottom of the page likely continues on the next spread.

pages.full_text:
We swam as soon as we got here. When we found our site at 7.45. we decided not to put up a tent.

Volcanoes of the Auvergne
				from west

[hand-drawn sketch of volcanic horizon profile]

July 19th
	We left straight after breakfast. We had intended to leave before breakfast, but the [?accueil] did not open until 9.0, & we had not paid. [?Simon] gave me tea at 7.45. We got up about 8.05. Sleeping bags & tables & chairs were away by 8.45.... So we had breakfast & left at 9.30.
	We stopped at Aurillac & bought Patisseries & changed money. It took 20 minutes for the bank to change money —
pages.route_sketch_description: Hand-drawn sketch in pencil of a horizon profile showing a series of gently undulating volcanic peaks, labelled 'Volcanoes of the Auvergne from west'. The sketch depicts a wide panoramic silhouette of the Auvergne volcanic chain as seen from the west, with a small figure or object (possibly a boat or person) in the middle distance below the skyline. No arrows or route lines — a scenic landscape sketch.
pages.notes: Page number at top right is partially cut off at the scan edge; reads as '85' based on position and the left page being '84', but only partially visible. The word before 'did not open until 9.0' appears to be 'accueil' (French for reception desk) — plausible in a French campsite context. The name '[?Simon]' is a tentative reading — could be a family member's name. The sketch is in pencil while the text is in blue/black ink. Year not stated; travelling through the Auvergne region of France with references to Aurillac and mention of changing money, consistent with a French trip. Nearby spread A_0042 shows July dates possibly 1992, supporting that year tentatively.
